body: So.... podiatrist appt today. Not ingrown. He said it was curved tho and cut it down. Not throbbing now,so we will see. When he cut it, most of the fungus is gone. The other toe still has some fungus tho. I'm still using anti fungal and soaking. So hoping maybe I got it early enough...who knows. He said he thought my feet looked good tho so here's hoping. Pictures of before and today in comments. Thanks to all on here, this group really helped me.
source_group: Toenail Fungus Support & Management

body: So good and bad. I went to the Dr today cause my left toe next to my pinky feels like someone is squeezing it. Sometimes real painful, sometimes not. I got an ingrown toenail yay! And of course that toe has fungus on it. Referral to podiatrist, just have to wait a couple wks which for a specialist as we all know is not bad. My PCP gave me antibiotics and told me to soak it too. At least it is a fairly easy fix! Good news....he said yo...
source_group: Toenail Fungus Support & Management

body: I had a partial ingrown toenail removed earlier today. I did not know it could *possibly* involve chemicals. I'm breastfeeding and am worried they used Phenol. Is there anyway to tell if this looks like they did? I called but of course the dr left already. Checked my chart and nothing at all is listed.
source_group: Toenail Fungus Support & Management

body: They quoted me 1500 for laser. does it even work, I have skin issues and circulation as well as fungus. Might just remove all my toenails. I can't take oral as I have liver disease. question tho, if I remove all my toenail, can I even get a medical pedicure if there is no toenail even there?
source_group: Toenail Fungus Support & Management

body: Ladies what shoes do you wear? I'm going on a cruise and am dreading it because I don't know what shoes to wear. My toenails are awful and to make matters worse I took off half of the big nails and it hasn't grown back at all so I can't just hide them with polish.
comments: Will they add a fake nail of the top half of the nail is missing? Thanks

I've been on several cruises but each time i was scared. I've always put on a fake toenail on the infected nail and then clip the fake nail with nail clippers into my regular nail shape then paint over it. What makes me scared is them popping off and has happened at dinner before.
I have a full set of acrylic toenails for events
Look into Keryflex

I went on a cruise and have the same issue. I went to my nail salon and she put acrylic on my bad nails and made them look great! I felt normal for the first time in years. When I got back, I took them off.
I have a friend that has fake big toenails put on every month at the nail salon. Maybe?
I paint over my manky toe nails with bright purple nail varnish
I did crocs for pool/beach. I hate crocs but hate my toes more lol. They do have fun colors. And for a wedding amazon has really cute closed toes heels! Also, saw some cute wedges for the cruise
source_group: Toenail Fungus Support & Management

body: Anyone dealt with tailors bunion.? I can feel the bone just a little down from my little toe & my little toe hurts on the side .. any at home relief ?
comments: I just had a Tailor's appear overnight, red and so sore I could not wear shoes. So doc gave me a steroid shot (painful!) but it really worked. Bone still protruding the same! How long does this steroid shot work?

Tailor's bunions tend to get callouses, which hurt. Callouses are prone to warts. If you get all 3 together you need to see a GOOD podiatrist. A wart can look like a callous and you think it's just a callous.
Get it fixed. My tailors bunion was so painful after working on my feet all day. Glad I did. Easy surgery
I use toe spreaders and a wide toe box shoe like Altra running shoes. Then use some ibuprofen to knock down inflammation. It then goes away, takes couple weeks but works.
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body: Has anyone in here had the 3D Bunion correction surgery ? If so, what was your experience like? Doing research before I schedule surgery for bone fusion that my foot Dr recommends. I have moderate/severe arthritis as well as the bunion. Not sure if this makes a difference
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body: Hi guys! I'm new to this group and don't have typical bunions. I believe I have tailor bunions because I have painful bones that stick out on the outside of my feet and my pinky toes basically piggy back on my other toes. They've started getting more and more painful. I have Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome so I have an extremely high arch and tiny (non-existent) heels. I'm trying to find tennis shoes that actually have a wide enough toe part but narrow enough heel.
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body: Hello! I'm trying to decide between surgical options or no surgery. I'm 57 years old and have lots of travel and other plans for being active. Problem: painful toes (15+ years) and metatarsal ball of foot (since last fall). Cause of pain: 1) toes stepping on the bottoms of their neighboring toes causing painful sores and 2) prominent metatarsals taking too much impact, leading to stress fractures.
comments: Alignment of the foot is dictated by connective tissue not the skeleton. If you debone a fish, it does not change form. The fact that 3 different doctors have 3 different surgical plans, for me, is a red flag.

Have you tried a shoe with a rocker bottom and a carbon plate or really stiff sole that it doesn't bend? I have walked on my little toe for many years and finally went to wide width shoes even though I don't need a wide width and my toes have naturally spread enough.

I had a Taylor's bunion and had surgery on it back in January of this year. During surgery, the surgeon broke my 5th metatarsal, shortened it and added a plate and screws along with a bone graft from my heel. I TOTALLY underestimated the recovery. Foot surgery is no joke.
You might consider doing one of the online foot programs like Gait Happens or Petra Fisher. There is a lot of free content. Wearing minimalist/barefoot shoes has shown me WHERE my gait issues are.
Your feet sound like mine. I use toe separators...you can find various ones on Amazon, and wear Chaco Z sandals and boots. If I wear them most of the time, I can wear other shoes some of the time.

ALL surgery carries risk. And that's the risk you take if anyone messes with your feet. Think long and hard about having surgery. It may make things worse. I've read far too many post-surgery foot horror stories.

I have a new diagnosis for my ongoing metatarsal pain - Morton's neuroma. According to my new doctor yesterday's MRI shows no evidence there was ever stress reactions/fracture as was my prior diagnosis.
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body: Hi guys! I'm really struggling with this I know it doesn't seem too bad but I'm in A LOT of pain. I walk for a living. Both feet are bad. But this one flares up a lot. It seems to almost pop after about 2 months and then flares up a month or so later what can I do to make the pain at least bearable
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

body: If you have permanent nerve damage as a result of foot surgery, is that a reflection on the surgeon's skill? (I assume it's permanent after 6 months. 3 toes still numb. ). Trying to determine if I should be looking for another surgeon for my other foot as much as I hate to start over.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: I'm so happy. Went to podiatrist today and he took X-ray of my feet. Great circulation, no issues. Will take alpha lipoic acid tablets to help with nerve pain and come back in 2 months. I was worried about surgery! Relieved
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Does anyone use a tens device for nerve pain with success? Does anyone use a tens device for nerve pain!
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: what day was your worst pain? Had surgery yesterday morning and today the nerve block wore off, OW! I am taking vicodin. When did your pain start to get better?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Just got home from chevron akin bunion correction and 2nd metatarsal osteotomy. Nerve block so no pain yet. I was in pain before the surgery, just walking was near impossible so really happy to get this fixed and be on the road to recovery. Posting a before and after surgery picture.
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Can you walk in these type of night splints? I have to get up to use the rest room, but most, you get up and walk on them they fall or lose the secureness of them. I know you can walk on a boot so are these kind the same concept? Not like to purposely walk on them, but to at least go to the bathroom in the middle of the night?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: I have had heel spurs with plantar fasciitis under both feet for 20 months now. I have tried a night splint, 8 shockwave treatments, 5 osteopath visits, orthopedic insoles, stretching and strengthening exercises (including with a ball), cooling, and massage devices. Nothing helps; I am at my wit's end. Do you have any tips? Or good stories, because I am afraid this will never go away. I am a young woman of 29.
comments: I saw some where in the group comments about copper supplements or copper peptides. I chose the copper peptides.
One dose of the copper peptide I woke up pain free. Not even joking. I took another dose this week. I just don't want it coming back. It's an anti-inflammatory, and a healing agent.

I dealt with this for 6 years. By what you have written, you're not allowing your feet to heal. Stop stretching your fascia and sleeping in splints. Do reverse stretching on your foot, stretch the top of your foot, not the bottom.
Cut out all sugar and go on an anti inflammatory diet.
Dr. Eric Berg on YouTube did more for me than any podiatrist I ever saw.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Almost fully recovered thanks to my night splint. Next up: 10 day trip to Paris this summer. I have very flat feet, so a little lift in my shoes works wonders. Experienced flat footers weigh in!!!! What sandal would you recommend for flat feet and somewhat stylish.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: is night splint a big help for you guys? planning to buy those
comments: Yes a good night splint will give you relief from morning pain.
If you have a tear, worst thing you could do.
Castor oil massage and cotton sock on for night, every night

No made my PF worse. Castor oil and magnesium oil together with a sock on. Also deep tissue massage on calves or else use a massage gun as tight calf muscles cause PF. My PF didn't get better until I did this. Pain now gone by 90%.
Strengthen your glutes and hips and legs

Yes! But I cannot wear it all night. I put it on about 3 hours before my alarm goes off. Then I'm actually able to get out of bed. I walk around in it for just a bit. After taking it off, I put on this little strap on heel cushion to walk around in the house.
Yes! But this one the only one I can stand to sleep in. Barely notice it
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: While a night splint does seem to help, it's bulky and uncomfortable. My sheets are loose, but my feet are pointed down while I sleep and that seems to aggravate BOTH calves. Are y'all using anything while you sleep that keeps your feet more towards 90 degrees?
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: So I've been in pt for a while. I have two appointments for next week that were made on a let's see how I feel after my surgery today. I'm weight bearing and can walk but am in an ace and walking shoe until my follow up in two weeks to get stitches removed from my toe. Dr said I can go to pt next week if I'm feeling up to it. He wants me up and walking. Pt says I have to be reevaluated. How do they do that if I'm in an ace bandage. I don't know what to do. I'm so confused
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: How do you take a shower when 50% weight bearing BUT allowed to get foot wet?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: For non-weight bearing, how did you navigate getting from the shower to a place where you could get your boot back on? Did you do it all from the shower? I can only minimally heel walk in my boot.

body: Having osteomy in two toes done Thursday after having an akin bunionectomy on December 18th. Wish me luck as this time im non weight bearing. Momma with toddlers how did you do it? Luckily I live with momma. But any tips would be appreciated
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: I am 6 weeks post op, and one of my incisions is bleeding slightly. I was just able to be non weight bearing yesterday. Has this happened to anyone else?
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: What is everyone's thoughts on a possible link between diet and PF? Just wondering.
source_group: Foot Pain Community

body: Help! Ive been seeing a foot Dr for two years now and am on 600mg lyrica. Here's what an xray say: Minimal calcaneal plantar spurring. Question small chronic erosive changes of the distal 1st metatarsal possibly a sequelae of gout. Joint: No dislocation. Mild 1st MTP joint space degeneration minimal hallux valgus. Soft tissues: No ankle joint effusion.
source_group: Foot Pain Community

body: Greetings! Two questions: 1. Occasional big toe joint pain, but doesn't fit any description of gout that I've found. Throughout any given day, multiple times a day, it will lock up. When it happens, it is pretty painful, but if I force it up by pushing it against something, the pain immediately subsides. No swelling, no redness, no sensitivity of the skin... just the joint. 2. My old orthotic insoles are falling apart. What's the conventional wisdom these days regarding insoles?
source_group: Foot Pain Community

body: Added another photo, its just getting worse. Anyone know what this is? I have been seeing a podiatrist for a year now. Had a torn plantar plate about 5 or 6 years ago, had that repaired (2nd toe). Started having pain in the ball of my foot (big toe). Went in and they said there was a fractured sesamoid. Was in a boot for a couple months. Ive been in and out trying to figure out what is causing the swelling and pain that is steadily getting worse. Ive done custom orthotics.
source_group: Foot Pain Community

body: I am incredibly frustrated and depressed about my feet. I feel like no one even knows what's wrong with them. Going on 7 years battling pain in the Tops of my feet. Resting them only makes them better until I start using them again. I've tried gabapentin (didn't work plus I hated how it made me feel). I've tried several pain meds that barely help, but also can't be on forever. Had xrays and even two emgs (testing nerves).
comments: Have your heel fat pads ever been measured? Heel fat pad atrophy is a common yet VERY underdiagnosed condition
Same sinking boat. Following for advice. Quite literally feel your pain. I'm only 50

I took a recommendation of using B6 vitamin. I'm on day four using B6 and B12. Day three I felt a difference and today my feet don't feel so thick/inflamed/sore. On second day I also started watching how many oxalates I consumed (spinach, cashews, nuts, parsley etc) as that can be a cause of sore feet too.
I made a cayenne pepper balm for my husband's feet. Really does work on the pain. Recipes online

I had Rheumatoid Arthritis Bone Spurs Heel Spurs Plantar Fasciitis Haglunds Deformity Tendonitis heel Spurs Gout I could barely walk and always in pain I took all the meds and did all the therapy. Four years later using canes knee scooter. I had more tests and needed a Parathyroidectomy.
Yes!!! Four years here of similar pain. I've recently started the new Stemwave Therapy and have been pain free, it's literally a miracle!!
I often stand up and it feels like all the bones are crunching together across the tops of my feet. I can't wear laces in trainers anymore. Walking is unbearable after about 7-10 mins.
source_group: Foot Pain Community

body: 3 weeks post op this week. Wounds healing nicely and pain so much better. I'm celebrating as I got into these today! Surgeon said I could try, these are extra wide fit, but in my own size. So so happy to be out of the dreaded sandal and my foot feels much better walking in these. The only issue I have is the bunion on my left foot is now actually sorer than the operated one
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Ive had complex foot surgery on 11th March and now having problems with my healed wound. My gp has given me antibiotics. Any advice..

body: AI's Answer To Supplementation After Foot Surgery. This is NOT medical advice! I asked AI what supplements would be helpful after foot surgery when bone fusion and harvesting were involved.

body: Hello. My name is Michelle and I am in Oz. 4 weeks post op from 2 surgeries at once
source_group: bunion surgery / foot surgery support group

body: Update: results from biopsy came back as a benign scar!

body: Just Got surgery last Monday the 18th pain was very manageable with meds. I took oxy for 3-4 days then just used Motrin and nerve blocker. I take Calcium K2 and D3 daily they all work together to get into system and to bones and joints for healing. I did step on foot yesterday with a little discomfort after uhhhh big toe gets swollen when I'm up too long I washed my hair today and showered guess that was bit too much. I sleep with leg elevated.
source_group: Minimally invasive bunion surgery

body: Not gonna lie, this pain has been some of the hardest I've ever had to deal with. I go for my dressing change today and am excited but also nervous to find out if it's healing the way it should be. I'm grateful because my doctor prescribed more strong pain meds so I'm relieved about that.
source_group: Minimally invasive bunion surgery

body: Last Wednesday I had my MIS surgery, the first two nights were very painful but by my follow up 7 days later I was feeling pretty good. (Still icing and elevating a lot). Unfortunately x-rays showed one of the screws had become loose so I had another surgery this morning. The pain is not so tolerable. My doctor prescribed a host of meds my first go around- what combo and timetable was helpful for you? Toradol, Norco, Gabapentin.
source_group: Minimally invasive bunion surgery

body: Tomorrow makes 3 months post op. Next appt is in 8 days. I'm STILL in a walking boot with an ace bandage. What we think is CRPS sucks. Pic is of my foot once I get out of the shower. All day it hurts in one spot, burning in another and starts over.
source_group: Minimally invasive bunion surgery

body: Update: still in the boot with crutches, don't know when I can stop. I get to start gabapentin (for the second or third time in my life now) for CRPS and I can try to weight bear with the boot. 1 month post op tomorrow! I've been in a boot with crutches since day one!
source_group: Minimally invasive bunion surgery

body: Went to the store specializing in all sports and running shoes. Tried every single brand like hoka, altra, topo, brooks, etc. New balance 1080 felt amazing so I got them. Was walking in them around the house the rest of the evening and next day did a few errands with not much walking but mostly driving. So while driving my feet started throbbing, I thought the shoes were too tight, so I adjusted them but feet were still throbbing.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Which shoe model is the best and has helped you the most for plantar fasciitis (Hoka, Asics)?

body: Hiya, I went to Skechers yesterday and saw that they have arch fit sandals. Has anyone tried these? We are off to Disney Florida again next year and I can't be in the same amount of pain I was in last year again. I was in bits. Trying to get an apt with my doctor atm is a pure mission too!
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Sketchers on sale at Belk. Does anyone get relief from Sketchers? Curious to see if they have helped anyone.
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Can I have some suggestions for somewhat cute flip flops/sandals for the summer?
comments: Birks work for me.
Reef sandals also have been supportive

I'll say in advance that I think everyone has different needs depending on what the structural issue is (flat feet vs high arches). Fit Flops have been the best for me. Changed my life honestly. I wear their flip flop type shoes, sneakers and boots.
I have a rec for one of each - true flip flip: Archie's (from Australia) and a Havaiana's slide (from Brazil). Have walked miles unplanned in each, including in the rain, mud, and forest trails.
I just bought some Skechers Slip-in ones that are super comfy too. Your best bet is to try on a whole bunch to find which feel best for you!
These look decent, are very comfortable, and have clinical arch support. PowerStep Archwear Women's Arch Supporting Thong Sandal.
I think birks are cute! They're my go to summer sandal
source_group: Plantar Fasciitis Talk and Tips Support Group

body: Has anyone ever been advised to use a Pointer Plus acu-point to manage symptoms? I have a rigid big toe, which is likely the cause of my symptoms (metatarsalgia and sesamoiditis...still trying to diagnose). My osteopath suggested this device so I could try to manage on my own rather than paying $190 to see him every couple of weeks. He also showed me how to manipulate my big toe to keep it from getting locked up. He's not typically someone that suggests stuff like this.
source_group: Forefoot Forum: Bunions, Hallux Limitus, Tailor's Bunion, Hammer Toes

source_group: Bunion Support Group

body: Post op day 1. Hammertoe repair and chevron bunion osteotomy. Nerve block still doing it's work. Not too much pain. Heel walking in ortho shoe with cane to bathroom. Elevation and ice. Cryotherapy is helpful. Weil procedure wire freaks me out a bit but it is what it is. More to come..
source_group: Bunion Support Group

body: Second bunion surgery on both feet! I had the Chevron surgery on right foot today 10/16/25. Lapidus procedure on left foot done 3/20/25. Long recovery!

body: My surgery is next Wednesday on my left foot (eventually I'll do the right foot). My doctor will be performing a chevron osteotomy along with a dorsal cheilectomy. I'm curious how things have gone for you if you've had this procedure or something similar?
source_group: Bunion Support Group

body: 9 days post op from bilateral chevron bunionectomy. Still have pins in for a few more weeks. No pain except when walking bc of pins and swelling. Hang in there if you're just getting started!
